skill.name: competitive-ads-extractor
skill.description: Extracts and analyzes competitor ads from public ad libraries to identify messaging themes, pain points, creative patterns, and campaign ideas.
skill.summary: Analyze competitor ad libraries and turn public ad examples into marketing insights.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and appear to be false positives. The external command matches are markdown code fences containing example prompts, and the weak cryptography matches are ordinary prose substrings. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable code, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged fenced markdown prompt examples and sample output blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation examples, not executable code, and I found no command invocation path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are visible markdown code fences around natural-language prompts and example output. There is no script file, interpreter directive, or user input passed to a shell.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are Prose
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography at lines that contain the skill description, an insights bullet, and ethical guidance about designs. I found no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive transformation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary documentation text. The file contains no implementation code where a cryptographic algorithm could be used.

content.value_statement: Competitive ad research is slow when teams inspect each library manually. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to collect public ad examples and summarize useful marketing patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guides extraction of ads from public ad libraries such as Facebook and LinkedIn.
Captures competitor messaging themes, pain points, offers, and calls to action.
Groups ads by theme, audience, platform, format, or creative approach.
Summarizes repeated copy and visual patterns for campaign planning.
Suggests original ad angles based on observed competitor patterns.
limitations: It depends on public ad library access and platform availability.
It does not verify actual ad performance unless the platform exposes metrics.
It cannot guarantee complete coverage of every active or historical ad.
Users remain responsible for legal, ethical, and platform compliance.

use_cases.title: Map Competitor Messaging
use_cases.description: Compare active ads from several competitors to identify common promises, pain points, and positioning gaps.

use_cases.title: Plan New Campaign Tests
use_cases.description: Turn public ad examples into original campaign hypotheses, message angles, and creative briefs.

use_cases.title: Build a Swipe File
use_cases.description: Create a structured reference library of ad screenshots, copy themes, formats, and calls to action.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One Competitor
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract current public ads from [Competitor] on [Platform]. Summarize the main themes, offers, pain points, and calls to action.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Competitor Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze public ads from [Company A], [Company B], and [Company C]. Compare their audiences, promises, creative formats, and positioning.

prompt_templates.title: Find Messaging Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review ads from [Competitor List] for [Product Category]. Identify repeated claims, underserved pain points, and original angles we could test.

prompt_templates.title: Track Changes Over Time
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare saved ads from [Competitor] across [Time Periods]. Explain how messaging, formats, offers, and audiences changed.

output_examples.input: Analyze Facebook ads from a productivity software competitor.
output_examples.output: A concise report listing active ad themes, main pain points, common calls to action, creative patterns, and campaign ideas.

output_examples.input: Compare LinkedIn ads from three B2B SaaS companies.
output_examples.output: A side-by-side summary of positioning, target audiences, proof points, offer styles, and gaps for differentiated messaging.

output_examples.input: Review saved competitor ads from Q1 and Q2.
output_examples.output: A trend summary showing which themes increased, which offers disappeared, and which formats deserve testing.

best_practices: Use public sources and follow each platform's terms and access limits.
Adapt patterns into original creative instead of copying competitor ads.
Repeat the research on a schedule to detect messaging changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to plagiarize copy, designs, or brand assets.
Do not treat ad frequency as proof of performance without supporting data.
Do not ignore legal, privacy, or platform rules during collection.

faq.question: Does this skill run a scraper by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow for extracting and analyzing public ad library information with the tools available in your environment.

faq.question: Which platforms does it mention?
faq.answer: The skill references Facebook Ad Library and LinkedIn, with room to compare other public ad sources.

faq.question: Can it identify winning ads?
faq.answer: It can identify repeated patterns and likely strengths, but it cannot confirm performance without reliable platform metrics.

faq.question: Can I copy competitor ads directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends research and inspiration only, with original copy and creative for your own campaigns.

faq.question: What outputs can I request?
faq.answer: You can request summaries, screenshots, spreadsheets, presentations, pattern libraries, and campaign recommendations.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives Claude Code and Codex a structured marketing research workflow for public ad analysis.
